Nuno Felix We are a founding member of NATO. We fought a 3 theater colonial war from 1961 to 1974. The United States actively armed some of the Rebels, same as the Soviet Union (different rebels). NATO was never an issue You're simply ignorant. In Operation ELDORADO CANYON, Reagan's 1986 bombing of Libya, NATO members France, Spain and Italy all denied DoD use of bases or their airspace, which made the operation more difficult. Nobody whined about ending NATO over it. - John Schindler tweet

🇺🇸 Houston, We Have a Smell Now halfway to the Moon, the Artemis II crew has reported their second toilet issue of the mission. Astronauts say there is a burning, heater-like smell coming from the toilet, though mission control is not concerned. The first problem with the $23 million Orion space toilet was reported just hours after launch, when a fan was found to be jammed. The crew was able to fix the issue with guidance from mission control. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

They discussed ways to strengthen bilateral cooperation and coordinate on regional developments amid continued Iranian missile and drone attacks on Bahrain and GCC countries. Both stressed the importance of joint diplomatic efforts in international forums, including their current non-permanent seats on the UN Security Council. @wfwitness

OSINTdefender India’s Oil and Natural Gas Ministry has announced the purchase of both crude oil and liquefied petroleum gas from Iran amid the ongoing war in the Middle East and the blockage of the Strait of Hormuz, constituting the first purchase of energy products from Iran in several years by India. This announcement follows last month’s lifting of sanctions levied against Iranian tankers by the U.S. Treasury Department. tweet

Open Source Intel Lindsey Graham: I just had a great conversation with Trump. I totally support his ultimatum to the Iranian regime to open up the Strait of Hormuz and to do a peace deal. A massive military operation awaits Iran if they choose poorly. This regime has been severely crippled through Operation Epic Fury. Their reign of terror against the region and the world needs to come to an end, hopefully through a peace deal. After speaking with President Trump this morning, I am completely convinced that he will use overwhelming military force against the regime if they continue to impede the Strait of Hormuz and refuse a diplomatic solution to achieve our military objectives. If it’s not clear to Iran and others by now that President Trump means what he says then I don’t know when it will ever be. Choose wisely. tweet

🇫🇷 🗣 Politico: France intends to increase its stockpiles of missiles and drones by four times by 2030 The newspaper notes that next week the French government will present an updated law on military planning, which provides for the allocation of €8.5 billion for drones and missiles until 2030. The document represents a multi-year financial framework that defines the goals of arms procurement and development. France plans to increase its stockpiles of cruise missiles, including "kamikaze drones", by 400%, guided bombs AASM Hammer produced by Safran - by 240%, and Aster and Mica missiles - by 30%. Also, the publication reports that Paris is considering the development of a new tank, which is supposed to replace the Leclerc tank. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 🇺🇳 Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has written to the UN Secretary‑General and the Security Council detailing repeated US‑Israeli attacks on Iranian nuclear facilities, including strikes on Natanz (1 and 21 March), the Bushehr plant (24 and 27 March, plus a nearby strike on 17 March), the Khondab heavy water plant and the Shahid Ahmadi‑Roshan uranium processing site. He warned that attacks near the active Bushehr reactor risk a radiological release with catastrophic human and environmental consequences for the entire region. Araghchi criticised the IAEA Director-General Grossi for “dangerous” remarks suggesting the use of nuclear weapons against Iran, and said the agency’s inaction has encouraged further aggression. He warned that member states may lose confidence in the UN, the IAEA and the non‑proliferation regime if attacks on safeguarded facilities continue to go unpunished. @wfwitness
